basics.description:
![Abstract Security](https://cybersecurity-excellence-awards.com/wp-content/uploads/163661.png)

# ABSTRACT SECURITY
**The Security Data Pipeline Platform.** Stream, reduce, enrich, and route your security telemetry on your terms — no lock-in, no waste.

[abstract.security](https://abstract.security) · [Documentation](https://docs.abstractsecurity.app/docs/integrations/source-integrations/azure-event-hub/) · [Book a demo](https://abstract.security)

---
### Azure Event Hub — Source Integration
Abstract **reads from** Event Hub. This guided deployment provisions everything the integration needs: an Event Hubs namespace, one Event Hub per log source, the `abstract` consumer group, the required checkpoint Storage Account + private blob container, and your choice of Connection String and/or Service Principal authentication — with Safe Mode networking so onboarding never breaks.

✓ Production-ready  ✓ Least-privilege by default  ✓ No secrets in outputs

When the deployment finishes, the **Outputs** tab is your field-for-field answer key for the Abstract integration modal.

basics.name: namespaceName
basics.label: Event Hubs namespace name
basics.toolTip: Globally unique, 6-50 characters, letters/numbers/hyphens, starts with a letter. Becomes <name>.servicebus.windows.net.

constraints.validationMessage: 6-50 chars, letters/numbers/hyphens, must start with a letter and end with a letter or number.

basics.name: sku
basics.label: Pricing tier
basics.toolTip: Abstract requires Standard as the minimum tier. Premium uses Processing Units and dedicated capacity.

basics.name: capacity
basics.label: Capacity (Throughput / Processing Units)
basics.toolTip: Sizing per Abstract docs: ~1 TU per 1 MB/s of expected ingress (1 MB/s = 1 TU, 10 MB/s = 10 TU, 32 MB/s = 32 TU). Premium accepts 1, 2, 4, 8 or 16 PUs only.

basics.name: autoInflate
basics.label: Enable Auto-Inflate (Standard tier scales Throughput Units automatically)

options.text: One Event Hub is created per log source, named <prefix>-<environment>-<source> (e.g. abs-prod-entra). Each hub gets the consumer group below — enter that consumer group in the Abstract integration. Abstract docs recommend at least 4 partitions; retention should cover possible ingestion downtime.

constraints.validationMessage: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens (max 21 chars).

elements.name: environment
elements.label: Environment token
elements.toolTip: Used in hub names, e.g. prod, staging, dev.

constraints.validationMessage: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens (max 21 chars).

elements.name: hubSourcesCsv
elements.label: Log sources (comma-separated)
elements.toolTip: One Event Hub per entry. Typical: activity (Azure Activity Log), entra (Entra ID sign-in/audit), defender (Defender XDR streaming), resource (Azure Resource Logs).

elements.name: defaultPartitionCount
elements.label: Partitions per hub
elements.toolTip: Abstract docs recommend at least 4. Partitions cannot be reduced after creation.

elements.name: defaultRetentionDays
elements.label: Message retention (days)
elements.toolTip: Size to ride out ingestion downtime or delay.

elements.name: consumerGroupName
elements.label: Consumer group for Abstract
elements.toolTip: Created on every hub. Enter this in the 'Event Hub Consumer Group' field of the Abstract integration.

constraints.validationMessage: 1-50 chars: letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, underscores.

options.text: Match this to the Authentication Method you will pick in the Abstract integration. Connection String creates a least-privilege SAS rule (keys are NEVER exposed in deployment outputs). Service Principal assigns Azure Event Hubs Data Receiver on the namespace and Storage Blob Data Contributor on the checkpoint storage account.

elements.name: sasRuleName
elements.label: SAS rule name
elements.toolTip: Least-privilege shared access policy created at namespace level. Use its connection string in Abstract instead of RootManageSharedAccessKey.

constraints.validationMessage: 1-50 chars: letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, underscores.

elements.name: includeSend
elements.label: Include Send right on the SAS rule (only if log producers will share this rule; Abstract only needs Listen)

elements.name: perHubSasRules
elements.label: Also create a per-hub SAS rule (tighter blast radius than the namespace-level rule)

elements.name: principalId
elements.label: Service principal object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the Enterprise Application (service principal) Abstract authenticates as — NOT the Application (client) ID. Create the app registration first (e.g. abstract-eventhub-ingestion) or let the companion script create it for you.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a GUID (the service principal OBJECT id).

elements.name: roleDefinitionName
elements.label: Event Hubs role
elements.defaultValue: Azure Event Hubs Data Receiver (Abstract docs)

allowedValues.label: Azure Event Hubs Data Receiver (Abstract docs)

elements.name: profile
elements.label: Security profile
elements.defaultValue: Safe Mode — public access stays on (recommended for onboarding)
elements.toolTip: Safe Mode guarantees the namespace and checkpoint storage stay reachable by Abstract while you onboard. Harden later by redeploying with IP Allowlist, Private Only or Hybrid.

allowedValues.label: Safe Mode — public access stays on (recommended for onboarding)
IP Allowlist — public, firewall default-deny with allowed CIDRs
Private Only — no public access, Private Endpoints required
Hybrid — Private Endpoint + public IP allowlist

options.text: Private Only blocks ALL public access. Abstract must then reach the namespace and the checkpoint storage account over your private connectivity (e.g. a forwarder inside the VNet). Verify this before choosing it, or logs will stop flowing.

elements.name: ipCsv
elements.label: Allowed public IP ranges (comma-separated CIDRs)
elements.toolTip: Abstract egress IPs plus your admin ranges. The storage firewall rejects /31 and /32 — list single addresses as bare IPs.

constraints.validationMessage: Comma-separated IPv4 addresses or CIDR ranges.

elements.name: subnetId
elements.label: Subnet resource ID for Private Endpoints
elements.toolTip: Full ARM resource ID of an existing subnet, e.g. /subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<vnet>/subnets/<subnet>

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a full subnet resource ID.
Must be the resource ID of a privatelink.servicebus.windows.net Private DNS zone.
Must be the resource ID of a privatelink.blob.core.windows.net Private DNS zone.

elements.name: allowAzureServices
elements.label: Allow trusted Microsoft services through the firewall (required for diagnostic-settings export when the firewall denies by default)

options.text: The Abstract Event Hub consumer requires a Storage Account with a private blob container for checkpointing and lease management. The container name goes in the 'Storage Blob Container Name' field of the Abstract integration.

elements.name: createStorageAccount
elements.label: Create the checkpoint Storage Account + container (uncheck only to bring your own)

elements.name: storageAccountName
elements.label: Storage account name (leave empty to auto-generate)
elements.toolTip: 3-24 lowercase letters/numbers, globally unique. Empty = auto-generated unique name (abs<hash>).

constraints.validationMessage: 3-24 lowercase letters and numbers, or empty to auto-generate.

elements.name: blobContainerName
elements.label: Checkpoint container name
elements.toolTip: Enter this in the 'Storage Blob Container Name' field of the Abstract integration.

constraints.validationMessage: 3-63 lowercase letters, numbers and dashes; starts/ends alphanumeric.

elements.name: storageAllowSharedKeyAccess
elements.label: Allow shared-key access (REQUIRED for Connection String authentication; uncheck only for pure Service Principal deployments)

elements.name: storageApplyIpRules
elements.label: Mirror the Event Hub IP allowlist onto the storage firewall

elements.name: createStoragePrivateEndpoint
elements.label: Create a blob Private Endpoint for the checkpoint storage account

elements.name: enableDiagnostics
elements.label: Enable diagnostic settings on the namespace (monitor the health of the log pipeline itself)
